rulings.Case Title: THOMAS TAYLOR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2009, line 15, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's objection to newly filed Ay Declaration is sustained. The Court's tentative decision from the February 2nd hearing is the decision of this Court. As to the government contractor defense, defendant fails to shift the burden. Although the Navy may have been involved in testing the Unibestos, there is no showing that the government designed the Unibestos, or that the Unibestos was anything other than the product readily available to commercial users. See, In re Hawaii Federal Asbestos Cases 960 F2d 806 (9th Cir. 1992). =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT CONNIE FUNG, FUNG TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer to second amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. As pled, the claims appear to be barred by the applicable statute of limitations. (CCP section 340(a) and Menefee vs. Ostawari (1991) 228 Cal. App. 3d 239). =(302/CWW/X-A)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASON LHOTKA BY ELENA LHOTKA, et al VS. GEORAPHIC EXPEDITONS, INC., ALSO DOING BUSINESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Perpetate Testimony And To Inspect Documents Pending Appeal And For Issuance Of Commissions To Take Depositiions Outside The State Of California And In Tanzania, Africa
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFF THE ESTATE OF JASON LHOTKA BY ELENA LHOTKA,, ELENA LHOTKA, NICHOLAS LHOTKA, SANDRA MENEFEE'S Motion To Perpetate Testimony And To Inspect Documents Pending Appeal And For Issuance Of Commissions to Take Depositions Outside The State Of California And In Tanzania, Africa. Motion denied. CCP section 916 provides that "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court.... upon the matters enbraced therein or affected thereby..." The appeal denying the motion to compel arbitration affects the entire case. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GENARO PAED VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL BANK A BUSINESS ENTITY FORM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Reuben L. Nocos In Support Of Motion; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2009, line 16, PLAINTIFF GENARO PAED'S Motion To Consolidate Actions. Plaintiff Paed's motion to consolidate is Denied. The allegations stated in his complaint are properly presented in an action separate from the summary proceedings in unlawful detainer. The motion to consolidate is untimely. Trial in the unlawful detainer case is set for February 9, 2009, after being continued from January 12, 2009. Consolidating these actions would require delay of trial and prejudice to defendants, the current owners of the property that are seeking possession. =(302/CWW/VC)
